model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: UMBC holds a consistent edge over Howard across every major efficiency metric, ranking 22 spots higher in both KenPom (#185 vs. #207) and Torvik (#185 vs. #206), with a superior adjusted offensive efficiency (107.5 vs. 103.4) and a stronger overall AdjEM (-1.68 vs. -3.17). The Retrievers won both the America East regular-season title and conference tournament at 17-2, a meaningfully tougher conference than Howard's MEAC, giving them better schedule-tested preparation. Howard's 1-3 record in games decided by 3 points or fewer is a red flag in a tight play-in setting, and the betting market consensus of UMBC -1.5 to -2.5 aligns with the efficiency gap. While Howard's slightly better adjusted defense and solid 45.9% field-goal shooting keep this competitive, UMBC's offensive firepower and superior résumé make them the right side in this closely contested matchup.

reasoningStep.title: UMBC vs. Howard First Four Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: UMBC is the better team by every measurable metric — efficiency ratings, record quality, conference strength, and betting market — and is a modest but consistent favorite. Howard's defense and shooting percentage offer a credible upset path, but their poor clutch record and weaker schedule undermine confidence in an upset.
evidence: UMBC ranked #185 in both KenPom and Torvik (AdjEM -1.68, AdjOE 107.5); Howard ranked #206-207 (AdjEM -3.17, AdjOE 103.4), a gap of roughly 1.5 efficiency points.
ESPN pregame preview confirms UMBC -2.5 spread and O/U 141.5; Howard is 1-3 in games decided by 3 or fewer points, a concerning clutch record.
Betting market consensus: UMBC -1.5 to -2.5 spread, moneyline UMBC -120 / Howard +100 across multiple books.
UMBC won both the America East regular-season title (17-2) and conference tournament, a stronger mid-major conference than Howard's MEAC.
No significant injury concerns surfaced for either team heading into this First Four game.

The Bison are 13-3 against MEAC opponents and 10-7 in non-conference play. Howard has a 1/3 record in games decided by 3 points or fewer. The Retrievers are 17-2 in America East play. UMBC is second in the America East with 32.7 rebounds per game led by Jose Roberto Tanchyn averaging 5.7. Howard makes 45.9% of its shots from the field this season, which is 3.5 percentage points higher than UMBC has allowed to its opponents (42.4%). UMBC averages 8.1 made 3-pointers per game this season, 2.4 more made shots on average than the 5.7 per game Howard allows. TOP PERFORMERS: Bryce Harris is averaging 17.1 points and 6.9 rebounds for the Bison. Cedric Taylor III is averaging 18.4 points over the last 10 games. DJ Armstrong is shooting 42.0% from beyond the arc with 2.7 made 3-pointers per game for the Retrievers, while averaging 13.2 points. Anthony Valentine is shooting 51.6% and averaging 14.0 points over the last 10 games. LAST 10 GAMES: Bison: 9-1, averaging 84.2 points, 32.8 rebounds, 18.5 assists, 8.5 steals and 4.4 blocks per game while shooting 49.6% from the field. Their opponents have averaged 65.2 points per game. Retrievers: 10-0, averaging 79.8 points, 35.3 rebounds, 11.0 assists, 5.6 steals and 2.3 blocks per game while shooting 48.3% from the field. result.text: NCAA Tournament: First Four Odds & Picks (2026 March Madness) | BettingPros to prevent flash of incorrect theme on page load --> Skip to main content Skip to navigation Skip to footer Click to go to homepage Picks Odds Tools Research Leaders Click to expand More menu Home Games Picks Upgrade Menu Click to Find a bet ... Contest Click to expand picks panel Click to open Sharp AI NCAA Tournament: First Four Odds & Picks (2026 March Madness) by Mike Spector | @MikeSpector01 | Mar 16, 2026 by Mike Spector | @MikeSpector01 | Mar 16, 2026 Arguably, the best sporting event of the year starts on Tuesday as the 2026 NCAA Men’s Tournament kicks off with two First Four games, followed by two more First Four March Madness contests on Wednesday. Like always, the First Four pits two matchups of No. 11 seeds and two matchups of No. 16 seeds playing their way into the 64-team field. These First Four games often provide intense drama and serve as the perfect appetizer to the marathon of Round of 64 games we will consume on Thursday and Friday. Here are our two best bets for the First Four games and leans for the others. NCAA Tournament Odds NCAA Tournament Pick’Em Contest NCAA Tournament March Madness Bracket Optimizer NCAA Tournament Betting Trends Go premium to view top prop bets based on Cover Probability, Expected Value & Star Rating >> Best NCAA Tournament First Four Bets ( Odds courtesy of DraftKings Sportsbook ) No. 16 UMBC Retrievers vs. No. 16 Howard Bison UMBC is in the “Big Dance” for the first time since 2018, when it became the first No. 16 seed to beat a No. 1, and I expect it to have the chance to face a No. 1 seed again. The Retrievers are red-hot, having won 12 consecutive games, including their first two America East Conference Tournament games by a combined 37 points. They are winning by an average of 16.8 points over those 12 games, playing to the equivalent of the 86th-best team in the country during the winning streak. Ever since UMBC inserted 6-foot-10 Spaniard Jose Roberto Tanchyn into the starting lineup, the team has taken off, as it is 10.5 adjusted points per possession better with him on the floor. Teams are unwilling to attack the rim on UMBC as much with him patrolling the middle. The Retrievers allowed a 32.3% field-goal attempt rate at the rim through the first 21 games, but just 22.8% over the last 11 games (which ranks in the 98th percentile). UMBC should have a huge advantage inside against Howard, as it ranks in the 94th percentile for two-point attempts in the paint, and in the 96th percentile for efficiency on such shots. Though KenPom projects a 71-70 Howard victory, I trust in UMBC’s trio of guards, who all average in the double figures. Bet: UMBC -1.5 ( +1021 ) No. 16 Prairie View A&M Panthers vs. No. 16 Lehigh Mountain Hawks Prairie View A&M is 19-12 to the under despite playing at the 23rd-fastest adjusted tempo in the country. The under has cashed in eight of their last nine gam

| KenPom Rank | **#185** | #207 |

- UMBC won the **America East regular-season title AND conference tournament** (17-2 in conference), a stronger conference than Howard's MEAC
- UMBC's **offense is significantly better** (AdjOE 107.5 vs. 103.4), while Howard's defense (AdjDE 106.2) is actually slightly better than UMBC's (109.2)
- Howard shoots **45.9% from the field** — a positive sign — but their offense ranks #276 nationally by Torvik
- Howard is **1-3 in close games** (decided by 3 or fewer points), a concerning clutch record
- UMBC led by **Jose Roberto Tanchyn** (5.7 RPG) and strong rebounding (32.7 RPG, 2nd in AE)
- Betting market consensus: UMBC -1.5 to -2.5, moneyline near -120 — a modest but consistent edge
- No major injury flags surfaced for either team
